content: The human language learning concept of reflexion (as a vocabulary item taught to build active linguistic competence) can be mapped to LLM self-reflection mechanisms as a scaffolded training framework, where LLMs practice "vocabulary-level" self-critique of individual reasoning steps before building up to full reflection over complete problem solutions. This framing treats LLM self-reflection not as an emergent innate capability, but as a learnable skill that progresses from basic recognition of flawed outputs to advanced critical reasoning, just like human students learning to reflect on their own writing.
